title: Pods — What's next
text:
Learn about the
lifecycle of a Pod
.
Read about
PodDisruptionBudget
and how you can use it to manage application availability during disruptions.
Pod is a top-level resource in the Kubernetes REST API.
The
Pod
object definition describes the object in detail.
The Distributed System Toolkit: Patterns for Composite Containers
explains common layouts for Pods with more than one container.
Read about
Pod topology spread constraints
Read
Advanced Pod Configuration
to learn the topic in detail.
That page covers aspects of Pod configuration beyond the essentials, including:
PriorityClasses
RuntimeClasses
advanced ways to configure
scheduling
: the way that Kubernetes decides which node a Pod should run on.
PriorityClasses
RuntimeClasses
advanced ways to configure
scheduling
: the way that Kubernetes decides which node a Pod should run on.
To understand the context for why Kubernetes wraps a common Pod API in other resources
(such as
StatefulSets
or
Deployments
),
you can read about the prior art, including:
Aurora
Borg
Marathon
Omega
Tupperware
.
